title: Tasty Tender Malaysian Tobiko Tagine
description: A sweet and snappy Malaysian dish featuring tobiko, cooked in a tagine with a blend of cozy spices, and is FODMAP-friendly.
instructions: Heat the coconut oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine over medium heat.
Add the sliced onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the ground cumin, coriander, and cinnamon, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chicken breast and cook until it is browned on all sides.
Add the tobiko, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the water and bring the mixture to a boil.
Cover the tagine and transfer it to the oven, where it will cook at 300 degrees for 30 minutes.
Remove the tagine from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
